question: Will CPI rise more than 0.9% in March 2026?
reasoning: The March 2026 CPI data has been officially released by the BLS, showing a monthly increase of only 0.3% on a seasonally adjusted basis. This is well below the 0.9% threshold required for YES resolution. The market should resolve definitively to NO, making the true probability 0%.
key_factors: Official BLS data shows March CPI increased only 0.3% monthly
Market requires >0.9% increase for YES resolution
Data already released on April 10, 2026 at 8:30 AM ET
No uncertainty remaining - outcome is determined
risks: Potential data revision (though monthly CPI revisions are rare and small)
Misinterpretation of seasonal vs non-seasonal adjustment methodology
